prompt:
A 500 mL opaque plastic bottle with a secure dropper cap stands on a bench next to a measuring syringe and a small beaker of tinted nutrient solution. The bottle carries a simple downward arrow and droplet icon instead of readable text, clearly suggesting a “pH down” product. A few drops are shown mid-air or as tiny ripples in the beaker, implying that a measured amount is being added to lower reservoir pH rather than poured directly from the bottle.

The scene is set in a careful near-future solarpunk hydroponics workspace with gloves and safety goggles resting nearby, softly reminding of safe acid handling. Background elements include a hydroponic tub and lush plants, lit by soft, even lighting. The 512×512 image is photorealistic with subtle stylization, no logos or legible wording on the bottle, and a composition that keeps the pH down bottle, syringe, and beaker as the focal trio while the rest of the lab stays gently blurred.
